articles.description: Shiv Sena UBT MP Sanjay Raut tells The Hindu that they opposed it because the ‘BJP draped a pink saree on the delimitation bill’

articles.title: Quote of the day by Leo Tolstoy: ‘An arrogant person considers himself perfect. This is the chief harm of arrogance…’
articles.description: Leo Tolstoy explains that arrogance blocks growth by making people believe they are already perfect. True progress requires humility, self-awareness, and openness to learning, while arrogance prevents self-reflection and improvement in everyday life.

articles.title: Stalin stands exposed after PM Modi’s assurance on delimitation: Edappadi Palaniswami
articles.description: Pointing out that the share of Tamil Nadu in the Lok Sabha would go up from 7.18% to 7.23%, Mr. Palaniswami, in an interview with The Hindu, said that “this disproves what he [Mr. Stalin] has been saying.”

topStories.title: Widow, 86, back in France after being held by ICE
topStories.description: Marie-Thérèse had moved to the US after reuniting with her long-lost love, an American man who died in January.

topStories.title: Opposition defeated an attack on the Constitution, says Rahul Gandhi
topStories.description: Congress leader assures Prime Minister Narendra Modi of Opposition’s ‘unconditional support’ if the government wants to bring the 2023 women’s reservation law into force

topStories.title: Trump Says US And Iran Are Working Together To Clear Mines From Hormuz Strait
topStories.description: Last week, the New York Times reported that Iran was unable to fully reopen the Strait of Hormuz because it could not locate all the mines it laid in the waterway to block the passage amid war.

topStories.title: Schemes offering cash incentive to women takes centre stage is Bengal poll campaign
topStories.description: As the Bengal poll campaign has reached its peak, the contest is gathering pace between the Lakshmir Bhandar of the Trinamool Congress and the Matri Shakti Vandan Yojana of the Bharatiya Janata Party

topStories.title: Iran's 'Nuclear Dust': What It Means And Why Trump Wants It Moved To The US?
topStories.description: Trump says Iran will hand over its stockpile of highly enriched uranium buried beneath bombed nuclear sites - a move that could significantly curb Tehran's nuclear ambitions.

topStories.title: ‘Insult to Nari Shakti’: Amit Shah fumes at opposition as women's reservation amendment fails in Lok Sabha
topStories.description: Amit Shah said Congress and allies blocked the passage of the essential constitutional amendment for the law granting 33% reservation for women in legislatures.

topStories.title: Trump's big claim after Iran opens Strait of Hormuz: 'Has agreed to never close it'
topStories.description: Hours after Iran announced that the Strait of Hormuz is open for commercial vessels, US President Donald Trump on Friday (local time) said that the Islamic Republic has agreed to never close it again.

topStories.title: Iran To Close Hormuz Again If US Naval Blockade Persists: Report
topStories.description: Trump, however, claimed that Iran has agreed to never close the Strait of Hormuz again. "It will no longer be used as a weapon against the world," he said.

topStories.title: What we know about the Israel-Lebanon ceasefire
topStories.description: A 10-day truce between the two countries is now in effect, with Iran-backed Hezbollah voicing support, as negotiations continue between the US and Iran.

topStories.title: Controversy as Lebanese banker Sehnaoui is praised for supporting Israel
topStories.description: Trump envoy Morgan Ortagus says Antoun Sehnaoui is a committed Zionist, even as Israel attacked Lebanon.

topStories.title: Can Pakistan secure Iran-US nuclear compromise, as Trump says deal ‘close’?
topStories.description: Pakistan's civilian and army leaders shuttle between key capitals as Trump says he may visit Islamabad to sign a deal.

topStories.title: With US spy laws set to expire, lawmakers are split over protecting Americans from warrantless surveillance
topStories.description: Some lawmakers are calling for widespread reforms following years of surveillance scandals and abuses across successive U.S. administrations. But even if the spy law known as Section 702 expires in April, the government's spy powers will not automatically lapse.

dailyNews.description: Some lawmakers are calling for widespread reforms following years of surveillance scandals and abuses across successive U.S. administrations. But even if the spy law known as Section 702 expires in April, the government's spy powers will not automatically lapse.

dailyNews.title: FAA to probe viral incident of pilots making animal noises on ATC frequency: 'Conversations must be related to….’
dailyNews.description: The FAA is investigating pilots who made animal noises on ATC frequency, emphasising that communication must focus on safe aircraft operation. The incident occurred at Ronald Reagan Washington National Airport, with pilots reprimanded for seemingly doing fun.

dailyNews.title: Trump warns naval blockade will continue despite Iran opening Hormuz Strait - 'Full force'
dailyNews.description: Hours after Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi on Friday announced that the Strait of Hormuz has been declared completely open, US President Donald Trump said the naval blockade will remain in place until the US-Iran transaction is 100 per cent completed.

dailyNews.title: NBA star hits back at NYC Mayor Zohran Mamdani for blaming him over expensive playoff tickets
dailyNews.description: Trae Young fired back at Zohran Mamdani after being blamed for expensive New York Knicks playoff tickets, despite no longer playing for the Atlanta Hawks.

dailyNews.title: Virginia Dems accused of illegally ‘steamrolling’ state law that could upend redistricting crusade
dailyNews.description: Virginia voters face a redistricting referendum Tuesday as a Supreme Court challenge argues the amendment was passed through an illegal special session.

dailyNews.title: Your favorite snacks could be quietly wrecking your knees, new research suggests
dailyNews.description: New research suggests ultraprocessed foods may increase fat in thigh muscles, potentially raising the risk of knee osteoarthritis regardless of weight.

dailyNews.title: Obama urges Virginians to vote yes on redistricting measure that could give Democrats 4 more House seats
dailyNews.description: Barack Obama urges Virginians to vote yes on a redistricting measure that could give Democrats four additional House seats ahead of the midterms.

dailyNews.title: Rep Burlison demands FBI probe after top US scientists vanish or turn up dead
dailyNews.description: House Oversight member Eric Burlison demands FBI probe after a string of disturbing disappearances and deaths involving top U.S. scientists.

dailyNews.title: Turkish grad student who co-authored anti-Israel op-ed at Tufts self-deports after legal battle with DHS
dailyNews.description: Rumeysa Ozturk, a Tufts grad student whose deportation case was terminated by an immigration judge, has self-deported to Istanbul, sources say.

dailyNews.title: Yankees fans brawl in stands as team gets beat down by Angels amid frustrating skid
dailyNews.description: A fan brawl broke out at Yankee Stadium during the Los Angeles Angels' 11-4 win as the New York Yankees dropped eight of their last 11 games in a frustrating stretch.

dailyNews.title: Anger swells as Swalwell's 'open secret' with women went unreported for years
dailyNews.description: Eric Swalwell's sexual misconduct allegations were an open secret, critics say, as the media industry faces scrutiny for years of failing to report them.

dailyNews.title: Harris blames Trump for rising gas prices — after once saying they're the 'price to pay for democracy'
dailyNews.description: Kamala Harris blasts Trump over surging gas prices tied to the Iran war, but her 2022 remarks on fuel costs and democracy highlight a stark contrast.

dailyNews.title: Trump plan for Triumphal Arch moves step closer to reality
dailyNews.description: The U.S. Commission of Fine Arts reportedly offered preliminary approval to President Donald Trump's plan for a massive new monument in the nation's capital city.

topStories.description: Iran announced that the Strait of Hormuz is completely open for commercial ships for the remainder of the ceasefire. President Donald Trump also confirmed the news. NBC News' Matt Bradley reports on what this could mean for the future as peace talks with Iran could continue.

topStories.title: Trump expected to nominate ousted FEMA chief to lead agency again
topStories.description: President Donald Trump is expected to nominate Cameron Hamilton to serve as administrator of the Federal Emergency Management Agency, nearly a year after he was removed as its acting head.

dailyNews.title: Judge halts construction on Trump's White House ballroom
dailyNews.description: Judge halts construction on Trump's White House ballroom

dailyNews.title: White House holds press briefing
dailyNews.description: Watch live coverage as White House press secretary Karoline Leavitt holds a press briefing.

dailyNews.title: Iran says Strait of Hormuz "completely open" amid Israel-Lebanon ceasefire
dailyNews.description: President Trump has welcomed Iran's announcement that, at least while the Israel-Lebanon ceasefire holds, the Strait of Hormuz is "completely open" to all ships.

dailyNews.title: U.S. could send senior officials back to Pakistan for Iran talks within days, sources say
dailyNews.description: The Trump administration is considering a renewed diplomatic push that could send officials back to Pakistan within days, sources said.

dailyNews.title: Key details on Trump's latest Strait of Hormuz announcement
dailyNews.description: Iran and President Trump announced that the Strait of Hormuz will now be open, but questions remain about the flow of traffic in the key water passage. CBS News' Olivia Rinaldi reports.

dailyNews.title: Trump says Strait of Hormuz is open but U.S. blockade will continue
dailyNews.description: President Trump posted on social media claims that the Strait of Hormuz will be "completely open." CBS News' Natalie Brand reports.

dailyNews.title: Controversial surveillance program extended by House but only until April 30
dailyNews.description: The House OK'd a short-term renewal of a controversial surveillance program used by U.S. spy agencies, after Republicans revolted and refused President Trump's push for a longer extension.

dailyNews.title: Latest news after Iran and Trump claim Strait of Hormuz is "completely open"
dailyNews.description: Iran's Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi and President Trump have declared that the Strait of Hormuz is "completely open." CBS News' Charlie D'Agata and Olivia Rinaldi report.

dailyNews.title: Trump Is Urged to Act on Iranian Site Feared Impervious to Airstrikes
dailyNews.description: Little is known about Pickaxe Mountain, but some experts say it illustrates the impossibility of relying on force alone to prevent Iran from acquiring a nuclear bomb.

dailyNews.title: Rumeysa Ozturk, Tufts Student Held in Immigration Detention, Returns to Turkey
dailyNews.description: Rumeysa Ozturk, who was detained for weeks by the Trump administration after co-writing a pro-Palestinian opinion essay, has graduated and returned home.

dailyNews.title: The 27-Year-Old Diplomat Waging Trump’s Cultural War With Europe
dailyNews.description: Five years out of college, Samuel Samson has driven the Trump administration’s push to upend America’s postwar relationship with Europe.

dailyNews.title: Lutnick Says Canada Trade Deal Needs to Be Reworked Ahead of Talks
dailyNews.description: Howard Lutnick, President Trump’s commerce secretary, derided Canada’s trade strategy and said a North American deal needed to be reworked.

dailyNews.title: Trump Tries to Stop Drug Smuggling From South America. Drug Smugglers Invent New Tactics.
dailyNews.description: Authorities are using drones, troops and A.I. to crack down on cocaine smuggling. But gangs are finding new ways to move record amounts of drugs across the world.
